title: Alte Nationalgalerie - Quick Summary
items: **The Alte Nationalgalerie is the temple-like art museum on Museum Island,** opened in 1876 as the original home of the Nationalgalerie.
**Its shape was deliberate:** Friedrich Wilhelm IV imagined a sanctuary for art and science, raised on a plinth like a temple.
**The collection is strongest for 19th-century art:** Caspar David Friedrich, Adolph Menzel, Schinkel, Manet, Monet, Renoir, Degas and Rodin are key names.
**The building was badly damaged in World War II,** reopened partly in 1949/1950 and fully returned after a major renovation in 2001.
**In 2026, check current floor and exhibition notices before going,** especially around the Cassirer Impressionism exhibition.
